ai_research.headline: Agents are rewriting their own skills, Meta bought Manus AI for $2B, and 96% of enterprises now run agents

items.title: Memento-Skills: AI agents that rewrite their own code
items.body: New framework lets agents rewrite and optimize their own skills via reinforcement learning — boosting task success from 50% to 80% without full retraining. This is a real capability leap, not hype.

items.title: Meta bought Manus AI for $2B
items.body: Meta acquired Manus AI — the autonomous agent startup — for $2B this week. Paired with the Muse Spark model launch. Meta is going all-in on agents that handle real workflows, not chatbots.

items.title: Google Gemma 4 — best open model for agents right now
items.body: Gemma 4 (up to 31B params) is Apache 2.0, runs on edge devices, and benchmarks well for reasoning and multimodality. Top pick for OpenClaw agents where you want local inference.

items.title: Best models for agents in April 2026
items.body: Benchmarks this week: MiniMax M2.7 (self-improving), Qwen3.6-Plus (million-token context), GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro. Claude Opus 4.6 still tops for coding and agentic tasks.

ai_research.learn_today: Read the Memento-Skills VentureBeat piece — self-improving agents are closer than most people realize.

openclaw_agents.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Managed Agents, Microsoft ships governance toolkit, Perplexity up 50% revenue from agent pivot

items.title: Anthropic Claude Managed Agents — now live
items.body: Anthropic launched a managed product for enterprise agent deployment — handles orchestration and integration for you via API. Lower barrier for building complex multi-step agents. This is their direct answer to the OpenClaw market.
items.try_this: Check if Claude Managed Agents API fits any of your current multi-step workflows better than hand-rolled orchestration.

items.title: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit — open source
items.body: Microsoft shipped an open-source toolkit targeting OWASP's top AI agent risks. Monitors and controls agents during execution. Relevant as 94% of enterprises now report agent sprawl issues.
items.try_this: Worth scanning the OWASP AI agent risks list — some apply to OpenClaw setups too.

items.title: Perplexity revenue up 50% after pivoting to agents
items.body: Perplexity shifted from search to full AI agents and saw a 50% revenue surge. The lesson: agent-first positioning is monetizing faster than search/chat positioning.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Try swapping one Claude Haiku call in your agent stack with Gemma 4 locally — benchmark the quality difference for your specific use case.

tesla.note: Reuters scoop: Tesla quietly reaching out to suppliers about a NEW cheaper SUV model — not a Model Y variant, entirely new. Q1 earnings April 22. FSD v14.3 still rolling out. ~60 Cybercabs spotted at Giga Texas.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: Tied up in xAI reorg news — SpaceX SVP Michael Nicolls named xAI president. SpaceX IPO buzz growing alongside the leadership shuffle.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Internal memo: xAI reorganizing engineering team, Nicolls (SpaceX SVP) named president and acknowledged xAI is 'clearly behind.' X rolling out Grok-powered auto-translation and AI photo editor globally.

items.title: Check JustWatch this week
items.note: Search timed out this morning. Check justwatch.com for what's new — will have picks here tomorrow.

items.title: Perplexity's agent pivot is the marketing playbook
items.body: 50% revenue jump from repositioning as agents vs. search. The lesson for QuizzyDots and InstaClaw: 'daily quiz game' is a category. 'AI-powered daily habit' is a positioning. Which do you want to own?

items.title: 94% of enterprises run agents — that's your audience for InstaClaw
items.body: The average enterprise now runs 12 agents. They all have the same problem: governance, reliability, control. That's InstaClaw's pitch. The market is enormous and already bought in.

macro.mood: Quiet open. BTC holding $71K. TSLA flat ahead of April 22 earnings.
